top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on extreme hillclimb — single sustained climb, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Mt. Washington Hillclimb's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on extreme hillclimb — single sustained climb. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Mt. Washington Hillclimb's extreme hillclimb — single sustained climb forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 7 miles.
7 miles on extreme hillclimb — single sustained climb burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Base fitness for Mt. Washington Hillclimb. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
616ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Mt. Washington Hillclimb, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,678ft of climbing on extreme hillclimb — single sustained climb does to your power file on race day.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Mt. Washington Hillclimb. Train it until it feels boring.
At Mt. Washington Hillclimb, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
5-second power closes gaps and wins field sprints. At Mt. Washington Hillclimb, neuromuscular snap is a tactical tool, not just a finish-line move.

race_overlay.nutrition: Standard hydration and fueling. One bottle per hour minimum. Gel or bar every 45 minutes at race intensity.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Mt. Washington Hillclimb. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Anaerobic Capacity to prepare you for 7 miles of extreme hillclimb — single sustained climb in Mt. Washington Auto Road, Pinkham Notch, NH → Summit of Mt. Washington (6,288ft).
